channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Animal activists celebrate their first global 'Sanctuary Day'
item.description: It's been 40 years since animal advocates founded a sanctuary for farm animals in New York and California, and they say April 17 is their first global sanctuary day.
item.content:encoded: It's been 40 years since animal advocates founded a sanctuary for farm animals in New York and California, and they say April 17 is their first global sanctuary day.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Bottar

item.title: Hormuz reopens, but insurers aren't ready to sound the all-clear
item.description: Insurance for cargo and oil vessels stuck in the Strait of Hormuz has skyrocketed. How can insurers help us understand the realities of the war with Iran?
item.content:encoded: Insurance for cargo and oil vessels stuck in the Strait of Hormuz has skyrocketed. How can insurers help us understand the realities of the war with Iran?
item.dc:creator: Avery Keatley

item.title: Gasoline could drop below $4 in coming days
item.description: Gasoline costs should start to fall soon, although a full recovery to pre-war prices is expected to take months. That's assuming that peace holds and traffic flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
Gasoline costs should start to fall soon, although a full recovery to pre-war prices is expected to take months. That's assuming that peace holds and traffic flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz opens for commercial ships but the U.S. blockade continues on Iran
item.description: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz is open, following the start of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump swiftly responded that the U.S. naval blockade on Iran will continue.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz is open, following the start of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump swiftly responded that the U.S. naval blockade on Iran will continue.

(Image credit: Ibrahim Amro)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon. And, Trump nominates a new CDC director.
item.description: A 10-day ceasefire to pause fighting between Israel and Hezb
item.content:encoded:
A 10-day ceasefire to pause fighting between Israel and Hezb

(Image credit: Adri Salido)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: In China, swapping out an EV battery is as fast as filling up a gas tank
item.description: In China, some EV owners have the option to swap out a low battery for a fully charged one, instead of waiting at a charging station. The whole process only takes about three minutes.
item.content:encoded: In China, some EV owners have the option to swap out a low battery for a fully charged one, instead of waiting at a charging station. The whole process only takes about three minutes.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Six months after ceasefire with Israel, people in Gaza say recovery hasn't even begun
item.description: Six months into a ceasefire that promised an end to the war and a surge of aid for Gaza, people say recovery hasn't even yet begun.
item.content:encoded: Six months into a ceasefire that promised an end to the war and a surge of aid for Gaza, people say recovery hasn't even yet begun.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: UN looks for a short-term solution for moving fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.
item.content:encoded: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: How the conflict in Iran is testing the U.S.-European alliance
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with correspondents Greg Myre and Rob Schmitz about how the Iran war is affecting the U.S. alliance with Europe.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with correspondents Greg Myre and Rob Schmitz about how the Iran war is affecting the U.S. alliance with Europe.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: Israeli and Palestinian activists share a vision for peace in Gaza
item.description: Maoz Inon's parents were killed by Hamas in the Oct. 7 attacks. Aziz Abu Sarah's brother died after being tortured in an Israeli military prison. Their new book is The Future Is Peace.
item.content:encoded: Maoz Inon's parents were killed by Hamas in the Oct. 7 attacks. Aziz Abu Sarah's brother died after being tortured in an Israeli military prison. Their new book is The Future Is Peace.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley
